- Recent findings: - Initial state: blue solid at [20,18], checkerboard at [34,8], green dot at [40,10] - Current state: blue checkerboard at [20,18], solid at [34,8], green dot at [40,12] - Red bar is horizontal (same as initial)

- Row: 38-39, 39-40, 40-41, 41-42, 42-43 (5 possible row positions)
- Col: 8-9, 9-10, 10-11, 11-12, 12-13 (5 possible col positions)
The yellow dot is at [20,24]-[21,25] within the container [18,22]-[23,27]. The relative position is (2, 2), which is the center.
The green dot is currently at [40,10]-[41,11], which is also the center (relative position (2, 2)).
Maybe the goal is to move the green dot to a corner position, like [38,8]-[39,9] (top-left) or [42,12]-[43,13] (bottom-right).
Let me try moving the green dot to the top-left corner [38,8]-[39,9].

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move green dot to top-left corner of container
# Current: [40,10]-[41,11], Target: [38,8]-[39,9]
# Need: UP x2, LEFT x2
action(['UP', 'UP', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
print("Result:", last_action_result)
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r, max_r = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c, max_c = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Green dot: [{min_r},{min_c}]-[{max_r},{max_c}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
